Package nonprofitbookkeeping.model.budget

Class BudgetLine

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.model.budget.BudgetLine
public class BudgetLine extends Object
Represents a single line item within a budget. Each budget line is associated with a specific account and includes details such as the total budgeted amount, the periodicity of the budget (e.g., annual, monthly), and optionally, amounts broken down by period and an associated fund ID.

    Constructs a BudgetLine with specified details.

  • Constructor Details

    • BudgetLine

      public BudgetLine()
      Default constructor. Initializes fields to default values (null for objects, default for primitives like Periodicity). periodicAmounts is initialized to an empty list. periodicity defaults to Periodicity.ANNUAL.

    • BudgetLine

      public BudgetLine(String accountId, String accountName, BigDecimal totalBudgetedAmount, Periodicity periodicity, List<BigDecimal> periodicAmounts, String fundId)
      Constructs a BudgetLine with specified details.
      Parameters:
      accountId - The ID of the associated account.
      accountName - The name of the associated account.
      totalBudgetedAmount - The total amount budgeted for this line.
      periodicity - The periodicity of the budget amounts (e.g., ANNUAL, MONTHLY).
      periodicAmounts - A list of amounts for each sub-period, if applicable.
      fundId - The ID of the fund this budget line is associated with (optional).

  • Method Details

    • setPeriodicity

      public void setPeriodicity(Periodicity periodicity)
      Sets the periodicity for this budget line. Future enhancements could include validating the size of periodicAmounts against the new periodicity or resetting/clearing periodicAmounts. For now, manual management of consistency between periodicity and periodicAmounts is assumed.
      Parameters:
      periodicity - The Periodicity to set.

    • setPeriodicAmounts

      public void setPeriodicAmounts(List<BigDecimal> periodicAmounts)
      Sets the list of amounts budgeted for each sub-period. Future enhancements could include validating the size of this list against the current periodicity. For example, if periodicity is MONTHLY, this list might be expected to have 12 entries.
      Parameters:
      periodicAmounts - A list of BigDecimal representing amounts for each sub-period.

    • getAccountId

      public String getAccountId()
      Gets the ID of the account associated with this budget line.
      Returns:
      The account ID.

    • setAccountId

      public void setAccountId(String accountId)
      Sets the ID of the account associated with this budget line.
      Parameters:
      accountId - The account ID to set.

    • getAccountName

      public String getAccountName()
      Gets the name of the account associated with this budget line.
      Returns:
      The account name.

    • setAccountName

      public void setAccountName(String accountName)
      Sets the name of the account associated with this budget line.
      Parameters:
      accountName - The account name to set.

    • getTotalBudgetedAmount

      public BigDecimal getTotalBudgetedAmount()
      Gets the total budgeted amount for this budget line.
      Returns:
      The total budgeted amount.

    • setTotalBudgetedAmount

      public void setTotalBudgetedAmount(BigDecimal totalBudgetedAmount)
      Sets the total budgeted amount for this budget line.
      Parameters:
      totalBudgetedAmount - The total budgeted amount to set.

    • getFundId

      public String getFundId()
      Gets the ID of the fund associated with this budget line, if any.
      Returns:
      The fund ID, or null if no specific fund is associated.

    • setFundId

      public void setFundId(String fundId)
      Sets the ID of the fund associated with this budget line.
      Parameters:
      fundId - The fund ID to set. Can be null.

    • getPeriodicAmounts

      public List<BigDecimal> getPeriodicAmounts()
      Gets the list of amounts budgeted for each sub-period. The interpretation of this list depends on the getPeriodicity().
      Returns:
      A list of periodic amounts.

    • getPeriodicity

      public Periodicity getPeriodicity()
      Gets the periodicity for this budget line. This determines how the periodicAmounts are interpreted (e.g., monthly, quarterly).
      Returns:
      The Periodicity enum value.
